channel.title: The Mutapa Times
channel.description: Business and intelligence newspaper delivering curated Zimbabwean news from foreign press for the diaspora.

item.title: Econet’s Mafikizolo booking sparks backlash amid xenophobia tensions

item.title: Police Break Silence On Detective Who Died Days After Alleged Secret Visit To Police Training Academy @PoliceZimbabwe
item.description: ZRP Speaks On Detective Who Died In Accident Days After Allegedly Sneaking Into Police Training Academy The Zimbabwe Republic Police (ZRP) has responded to widespread social media claims surrounding the death of Detective Sergeant Milika Laison, saying the officer died in a road accident days after he allegedly sneaked into the Ntabazinduna Police Academy. The [...] The post Police Break Silence On Detective Who Died Days After Alleged Secret Visit To Police Training Academy appeared first on iHarare News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Iharare News

item.title: No More Hiding: Smart Traffic System Hunts Down Offenders @263Chat
item.description: By Kudzaishe Chimonera Harare Mayor Jacob Mafume says the city’s new smart traffic management system will improve the enforcement of traffic laws by using technology to identify offenders and issue fines directly to motorists. Mafume said once the database is fully operational, motorists who commit [...] The post No More Hiding: Smart Traffic System Hunts Down Offenders appeared first on 263Chat. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: 263chat

item.title: Culture drives inmate rehabilitation as ZPCS advances Vision 2030
item.description: Precious Manomano Herald Reporter CULTURE is emerging as a powerful tool in the rehabilitation and reintegration of offenders, with the Zimbabwe Prisons and Correctional Service (ZPCS) increasingly incorporating cultural heritage,... (via Ebusiness Weekly) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Ebusiness Weekly

item.title: Mthuli Ncube “Admits” Government Failure To Feed Citizens
item.description: By A Correspondent| In what many see as a clear admission that the Government has failed to guarantee food security... Continue reading "Mthuli Ncube “Admits” Government Failure To Feed Citizens" (via Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Zimbabwe News

item.title: Malloti And Hulungende Implicated In Kariba Hit And Run Accident Of Pregnant Woman
item.description: Police Speak After Malloti And Hulungende Linked To Kariba Crash Dancehall performer Malloti and her husband, Hulungende, have been implicated in a road traffic accident in Kariba that left a pregnant woman injured, with police saying investigations into the incident are still underway. The incident reportedly followed chaos at a show where Malloti was performing. [...] The post Malloti And Hulungende Implicated In Kariba Hit And Run Accident Of Pregnant Woman appeared first on iHarare News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Iharare News

item.title: NetOne Reaffirms Commitment to National Development @263Chat @NetOneCellular
item.description: State-owned mobile telecommunications operator NetOne has reaffirmed its commitment to driving Zimbabwe’s digital transformation agenda, declaring that it is “not a spectator in Zimbabwe’s growth story” but a “co-author” of the country’s digital future. The remarks were made during a keynote address delivered on behalf [...] The post NetOne Reaffirms Commitment to National Development appeared first on 263Chat. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: 263chat

item.title: Man, 36, in court for recruiting Zimbabweans to fight in Russia-Ukraine war
item.description: He is arrested at bus terminus while helping recruit to board bus to South Africa The post Man, 36, in court for recruiting Zimbabweans to fight in Russia-Ukraine war appeared first on Zimbabwe News Now. (via Zimbabwean News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Zimbabwean News

item.title: Ramaphosa open letter to South Africans: ‘Protest is both a right and a responsibility’
item.description: ‘We must reject the idea that acts of violence or intimidation are justified on the basis of a grievance’ The post Ramaphosa open letter to South Africans: ‘Protest is both a right and a responsibility’ appeared first on Zimbabwe News Now. (via Zimbabwean News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Zimbabwean News

item.title: The Great Train Heist: How a 20-Man Robbery Gang Stripped NRZ of US$10,000 in Minutes
item.description: The National Railways of Zimbabwe (NRZ) has been hit by a massive and daring raid, where a 20-member gang attacked a feeder station and stole US$10,000 worth of critical parts. This was not a random act of theft; it was a professional and well-coordinated operation. The attack happened on the night of 23 June 2026 [...] The post The Great Train Heist: How a 20-Man Robbery Gang Stripped NRZ of US$10,000 in Minutes first appeared on My Zimbabwe News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: My Zimbabwe News

item.title: Tungwarara reignites Zanu-PF power battle @bulawayo24 @ZANUPF_Official
item.description: Zanu-PF central committee member and businessman Paul Tungwarara has declared a return to his empowerment programmes in Manicaland, reigniting his rivalry with fellow businessman Kudakwashe Tagwirei a... (via Bulawayo24 News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Bulawayo24 News

item.title: Fear grips migrants ahead of SA protests @bulawayo24

item.title: Quiet Money, Deep Roots: How the Oak Foundation Backs Zimbabwe’s Communities
item.description: The Geneva-based Oak Foundation has funded Zimbabwean causes since 1983. In 2025 its Zimbabwe programme made USD 7.9 million in grants, backing palliative care, landmine clearance, regenerative farming and care for children who have lost parents. #Zimbabwe #Policy #ZimbabwePolicy #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Tapiwa Hillary Phillipo
